Three women, named Sarah, Rachel, and Michelle, were at the local grocery store wearing yoga pants. They were browsing the aisles, picking up fresh vegetables and fruits for their families. As they walked around, they noticed men who were working in the store turning their heads to catch a glimpse of them, which made the women feel a bit uncomfortable.

During their shopping trip, they discussed their marriages. Sarah had been married for five years and was feeling unappreciated by her husband. She mentioned that he rarely helped her with household chores, causing her stress. Rachel, on the other hand, shared that her marriage was in a rut, and she and her husband were struggling to rekindle the spark they once had. Michelle chimed in, saying that she was grateful for her supportive husband, but was struggling to find time for herself as a mom of three kids.

Despite their problems, the women remained supportive of each other and offered advice and encouragement. They continued shopping, filling their carts with food for their families, and left the store feeling more empowered and connected to each other.

As Rachel and Sarah were exiting the store, their friend Michelle greeted a man named Leroy, who hugged her back passionately. Rachel and Sarah were surprised by Leroy's sudden appearance, but they could tell that Michelle was happy to see him.

Leroy and Michelle talked for a few minutes, catching up on each other's lives. They spoke about their work and their families, and Leroy even asked how Michelle's husband was doing. After a few minutes, Leroy said goodbye to Michelle, giving her another hug before walking away.

Rachel and Sarah glanced at each other, noticing the passion in Leroy and Michelle's hug. However, they didn't say anything to Michelle, not wanting to make assumptions or come across as judgmental. Instead, they continued to chat about their own lives as they walked to their cars.

It's important to remember that it's normal for people to have close relationships and connections with others. While Rachel and Sarah may have been surprised by Leroy and Michelle's passionate hug, it is not necessarily indicative of anything inappropriate or offensive. It's also important to balance our observations with respect for other people's privacy and boundaries.
show how Rachel, Sarah, and Michelle each talk about this new "man" they ran into

As Rachel, Sarah, and Michelle walked to their cars, they continued chatting excitedly about their shopping trip and the items they had bought. Suddenly, Rachel asked Michelle about the man she had hugged passionately earlier.

"Who was that man, Michelle?" Rachel asked.

"Oh, that was Leroy. He's an old friend of mine," Michelle replied, smiling.

"An old friend? He looked like more than just a friend to me," Sarah teased.

Michelle blushed a little and said, "Well, we've known each other for a long time. But we're just good friends."

"You seemed pretty close," Rachel added.

Michelle shrugged. "We are. But I'm happily married to my husband, and Leroy knows that. I just love seeing him when I can."

Rachel and Sarah nodded, understanding where Michelle was coming from.

"How about you guys? Do you have any interesting men in your lives?" Michelle asked, teasingly.

Rachel and Sarah rolled their eyes and laughed. "Not at the moment," Sarah said. "Just my husband and our two kids."

"We're not as exciting as you, Michelle," Rachel added, grinning.

The three friends chuckled and hugged, saying their goodbyes before getting into their cars and driving off. Even though Leroy's appearance had distracted them momentarily, they continued to support each other and enjoy their close friendship.

The next week, Sarah and Rachel were at the grocery store once again, picking up some groceries for their families. As they were walking down the aisle, they noticed Michelle talking to Leroy by the bakery section.

Sarah and Rachel watched as Leroy and Michelle hugged and kissed each other on the cheek, happy to see each other again. But then, to their surprise, they saw Michelle get down on her knees in front of Leroy while he handed her something.

Rachel and Sarah glanced at each other, trying not to stare. They both tried to act nonchalant, not wanting to be rude. Eventually, Michelle and Leroy finished their conversation, and Michelle came back over to join Sarah and Rachel.

"Hey, guys," Michelle said, smiling.

"Hello," Sarah replied.

"I saw you talking to Leroy over there," Rachel added. "How's he been?"

Michelle hesitated for a moment, then said, "He's been doing okay."

"Anything going on between you two?" Sarah asked, trying not to be too nosy.

Michelle shook her head. "No, no. We're just friends. He was giving me something he owed me, that's all."

Sarah and Rachel nodded, although they both still felt a little uneasy. They didn't want to assume anything about Michelle and Leroy's relationship, but the kneeling they saw made them feel a bit uncomfortable. 

As the friends finished their shopping, they chatted about their families and their lives, but the image of Michelle kneeling to receive something from Leroy lingered in Sarah and Rachel's minds. They didn't know what to make of it, but they hoped that Michelle was happy and making good choices for herself.
